# Foundation AppShell Wave — DRAFT (2026-05-20)

**Status:** draft (do NOT import to ACP yet)
**Wave id:** `app-shell-foundation-20260520`
**Parent module:** `00-foundation`
**Generated by:** Opus, per `/tmp/monopilot-shell-gap-brief-combined.md`

## Purpose

The runtime app at `apps/web/` currently has:

- `app/layout.tsx` = `<html><body><RegisterSW/>{children}</body></html>` only,
- `app/[locale]/layout.tsx` = `NextIntlClientProvider` only,
- `app/[locale]/login/layout.tsx` = centred card (correct),
- `apps/web/proxy.ts` = edge auth/idle/i18n routing,
- `apps/web/app/_components/user-menu-language-picker.tsx` = existing language picker is orphaned,
- no AppSidebar, no AppTopbar, no PageHeader, no Settings subnav, no `(app)` route group, no scanner shell, no shell smoke test.

Every module's prototype at `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/<module>/shell.jsx` repeats the same desktop pattern:

```
Topbar (56px) — Logo · Search · RolePicker · Avatar
Sidebar (280px) — NavGroups (Core / Operations / QA & Shipping / Premium)
  SubNav (240px) (only on Settings + future module shells)
  Page content with <PageHead/> + <Section/>
```

Scanner (`prototypes/.../scanner/shell.jsx`) is a deliberate exception — `ScannerFrame` device chrome (390×844). It must NOT inherit the desktop shell.

This draft wave covers exactly those gaps and the route topology / parity gate
needed before any module-T3-ui task can verify itself.

## Full module navigation contract

The shell registry is now explicit for all 16 MonoPilot modules:

- `foundation`: platform/non-user-facing, excluded from desktop nav.
- `scanner`: scanner shell, excluded from desktop nav; T-134 owns `/en/dev/scanner` harness.
- `planning-basic`: sidebar item `Planning` → `/planning`.
- `planning-ext`: sidebar item `Scheduler` → `/scheduler`.
- Desktop sidebar modules: Settings, NPD, Technical, Planning, Scheduler, Warehouse, Production, Quality, Finance, Shipping, Reporting, Maintenance, Multi-Site, OEE.
- Dashboard is a shell-level item and remains in Core.

`APP_NAV_GROUPS` target: 5 groups / 15 sidebar items:
Core, Operations, QA & Shipping, Premium, Analytics & Network.

UI-138 is the anti-404 contract: every active sidebar link must resolve under the localized `(app)` shell or the task fails.

## Prototype labeling contract

T-137 creates stable foundation shell entries in `_meta/prototype-labels/prototype-index-foundation-shell.json` and `master-index.json`.
Every T3 shell task carries `prototype-backed`, `ui-parity`, `prototype_match: true`, `prototype_index_entry`, `prototype_index_ref`, and `prototype_anchors` metadata. Missing UI evidence remains fail-closed.

## Route IA notes (review fixes)

- Settings pages use one final topology: `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/(admin)/settings/*`, matching the existing `(admin)/settings/users` tree after T-133. No parallel `(app)/(settings)` settings UI layout.
- T-133 must inventory and merge existing collisions before moving files: `(admin)/settings/users`, `[locale]/(admin)/settings/users`, `(auth)/actions.ts`, `(settings)/schema/_actions`, and onboarding special routes.
- Runtime i18n files are `apps/web/i18n/{en,pl,uk,ro}.json`.
- Tailwind v4 shell tokens live in `apps/web/app/globals.css` via `@theme { ... }` and/or CSS variables; no task assumes a Tailwind config file.
- Scanner e2e harness URL is `/en/dev/scanner`; `_dev` is forbidden because underscored App Router segments are private/excluded.

## Cross-module placeholders (out of this wave, captured in coverage)

- SiteSwitcher dropdown: `14-multi-site/T-020` — topbar exposes a static
  `org.name` slot; the switcher widget lands separately.
- Permissions-gated nav items: `02-settings/T-130` — `UI-132` settings subnav
  renders all items behind a `TODO(rbac)` comment, never gates.
- Module-specific sub-navs (PlanNav, ProdNav, WhNav, …): each module's T3-ui.
- Mobile-responsive desktop shell (hamburger collapse): out of scope; documented
  as deviation in coverage and `T-135`.

## Closeout fail-closed policy (binding for all UI tasks in this wave)

Per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`:

- If Playwright / browser parity evidence cannot be produced for any reason,
  the task is **BLOCKED / needs_human**, not completed.
- `ui_parity_evidence_missing` is itself a hard-blocking closeout state.
- `closeout_requires` for every UI task includes `screenshots`,
  `screenshot_or_dom_diff_artifacts`, `visual_parity_evidence`,
  `prototype_reference`, `console_network_check`,
  `acceptance_criteria_status`, `test_commands_and_results`,
  `changed_files`, `git_status`, `deviations_from_prototype`.

## Not yet done (intentional)

- Tasks are NOT imported into ACP. `import-request.draft.json` is staged for
  later operator review (`stage_as: "draft"`).
- `00-foundation/manifest.json` (T-001..T-126 index) is NOT modified.
- No product code under `apps/web/` or `packages/ui/` is touched by this wave's
  authoring step. Tasks describe the future implementation; the workers
  execute it after promotion.
